📄 **Reports Generated Successfully!**

📁 **Location:** `/config/.haca_reports/`

**Available formats:**
• Markdown (.md)
• JSON (.json)
• PDF (.pdf)

---
*Created by H.A.C.A*

chat.title: Chat with AI assistant
chat.subtitle: Ask questions about your Home Assistant configuration, automations and HACA issues.
chat.greeting: Hello! I can help you analyse your automations, explain configuration errors, or suggest improvements. How can I help you?
chat.ex_audit: What's the health of my HA config?
chat.ex_create_auto: Create an automation to turn on entry light at sunset
chat.ex_debug: Why isn't my morning automation triggering?
chat.ex_history: Show history of light.salon since yesterday
chat.ex_dashboard: Add a weather card to my dashboard
chat.rate_limit: Please wait {wait}s before sending another message.
chat.setup_steps: Settings → Voice Assistants → [your agent] → ⚙️ → select HACA

config.haca_ignore_body: Add the label haca_ignore to any entity, automation, script, scene, device, area or helper in Home Assistant to exclude it from all HACA scans.
config.haca_ignore_how: Labels are managed in Settings → Entities / Automations / Devices / Areas.
config.general_settings_sub: General settings, thresholds and active issue types
config.detailed_logs: Detailed logs in Settings → System → Logs
config.persist_hint: To persist across restarts, add to
config.haca_ignore_info:
You can exclude any area, device, entity, automation, scene, script or helper from HACA scans by adding the haca_ignore label to it in Home Assistant.
Go to Settings → Labels to create the label, then assign it to the elements you want to ignore.
config.mcp_token_description: The AI Chat can execute actions directly in Home Assistant (create automations, debug, control entities…). For external MCP clients (Claude Code), provide your token below.
config.mcp_token_link_hint: Create a token in HA Profile →
config.mcp_token_privacy: The token is stored locally in HACA configuration and is not transmitted externally.
config.mcp_token_no_restart: No HA restart required after saving.
config.ha_repairs_hint: Push HIGH issues to Settings → Repairs
config.startup_scan_hint: Run a full scan when Home Assistant starts

types.helper_orphaned_disabled_only: Helper only used by disabled automations
types.template_now_without_trigger: Template using now() without time trigger

hints.device_id_in_trigger: Uses device_id which breaks if the device is re-paired. Switch to entity_id.
hints.device_id_in_action: Uses device_id in action — fragile after device re-pairing.
hints.device_id_in_target: Uses device_id in target — switch to entity_id for reliability.
hints.device_trigger_platform: Device trigger platform — consider entity-based trigger.
hints.device_id_in_condition: Uses device_id in condition — fragile reference.
hints.device_condition_platform: Device condition platform — consider entity-based condition.
hints.template_simple_state: Template can be simplified to a state condition.
hints.incorrect_mode_motion_single: Motion automation in single mode may miss events. Use restart.
hints.deprecated_service: Uses a deprecated service call — update to the new name.
hints.unknown_service: Calls a service that doesn't exist in your HA instance.
hints.no_description: No description set — add one for documentation.
hints.no_alias: No alias/name set — harder to identify in HA UI.
hints.duplicate_automation: Very similar to another automation — consider merging.
hints.probable_duplicate_automation: Possibly duplicated logic — review both automations.
hints.ghost_automation: References entities that no longer exist.
hints.never_triggered: Has never been triggered since HA started tracking.
hints.excessive_delay: Contains a very long delay — may block the automation queue.
hints.zombie_entity: Referenced in automations but no longer exists in HA.
hints.unavailable_entity: Entity is currently unavailable — check device connection.
hints.stale_entity: Entity hasn't updated in a long time — may be disconnected.
hints.god_automation: Very complex automation — consider splitting into smaller ones.
hints.potential_self_loop: May trigger itself recursively — check trigger/action entities.
hints.hardcoded_secret: Contains what looks like a hardcoded API key or password.
hints.sensitive_data_exposure: Sensitive data may be exposed in notifications or logs.
hints.high_complexity_actions: High number of actions — harder to maintain and debug.
hints.high_parallel_max: High parallel_max value may overload HA.
hints.script_orphan: Script is never called by any automation or other script.
hints.script_cycle: Scripts call each other in a cycle — may cause infinite loops.
hints.helper_unused: Helper is not referenced in any automation or script.
hints.compliance_unused_label: Label exists but isn't assigned to any entity, device, or automation.
hints.dashboard_missing_entity: Dashboard references an entity that doesn't exist.
hints.blueprint_empty_input: Blueprint has an input field with no value set.

compliance.all_good_subtitle: No naming, metadata or organisation issues detected.
compliance.info: The Compliance tab checks HA best practices: helper naming, area icons, automation/script descriptions, entities without area. These are different from classic HACA audit issues.
compliance.area_limit_notice: Only the first 150 entities without area are shown. Assign areas via Settings → Devices & Services → Devices to clear these issues.

mcp.hint_claude_code: Run: claude mcp add-json haca (paste the JSON below). No proxy needed, Claude Code supports HTTP natively.
mcp.hint_claude_desktop: Claude Desktop only supports stdio transport. Prerequisites: install uvx by running 'pip install uv' (or 'pipx install uv') in a terminal. Copy the JSON below into the config file: Windows: C:\Users\<YOU>\AppData\Roaming\Claude\claude_desktop_config.json (also accessible via %APPDATA%\Claude\) — macOS: ~/Library/Application Support/Claude/claude_desktop_config.json. Then restart Claude Desktop completely (File → Exit, not just close).
mcp.ip_warning: If homeassistant.local does not work, use the IP address of your Home Assistant server (e.g. http://192.168.1.x:8123). Some MCP clients cannot resolve .local addresses.
mcp.title: HACA MCP Server
mcp.subtitle: Connect Claude Code (or any MCP-compatible AI agent) to your HA: audit, automation creation, debugging, dashboards.
mcp.token_hint: Create a token in your HA profile →
mcp.hint_cursor: Cursor → Settings → Tools & MCP → + Add new MCP server | File: ~/.cursor/mcp.json
mcp.hint_vscode: Copilot Agent mode → .vscode/mcp.json (Ctrl+Shift+P → 'MCP: Open User Configuration')
mcp.hint_windsurf: Windsurf → Cascade → MCP Servers → View raw config | File: ~/.codeium/windsurf/mcp_config.json
mcp.hint_cline: VS Code → Cline extension → MCP Servers → Configure → cline_mcp_settings.json
mcp.hint_antigravity: Requires mcp-proxy. Install: pip install mcp-proxy. The -H flag passes the Bearer token to HACA.
mcp.hint_openwebui: Admin Panel → Tools → MCP → Add Server → paste URL and auth header
mcp.hint_n8n: Node MCP Client Tool → URL + Auth Header (HTTP/SSE mode)
mcp.hint_http: Any HTTP client: curl, Postman, Insomnia, etc.
mcp.guide_stdio_info: Claude Desktop only supports stdio transport. A bridge (uvx mcp-proxy) is required to connect to HACA's HTTP server.
mcp.guide_proxy_info: Antigravity / Gemini require mcp-proxy as a bridge to connect to HACA's HTTP server.
mcp.guide_restart_detail: File → Exit (not just close the window), then relaunch.
mcp.guide_antigravity_detail: In Antigravity: Settings → MCP Servers → Add → paste the JSON above. In Gemini CLI: add to ~/.gemini/settings.json.

agent.title: Proactive AI Agent
agent.subtitle: Background analysis, issue correlations, automatic weekly report and preference learning.
agent.report_sent: Weekly report sent to HA notifications!
agent.report_freq_hint: Checked once a day. Report is sent once the chosen interval has elapsed.

compliance_messages.no_friendly_name: Helper '{entity_id}' has no friendly name. Add a descriptive name in Settings → Helpers.
compliance_messages.raw_entity_name: Entity '{entity_id}' uses a raw entity name as friendly name: '{friendly_name}'. Replace with a readable name.
compliance_messages.area_no_icon: Area '{name}' has no icon. Add an icon in Settings → Areas.
compliance_messages.unused_label: Label '{name}' is defined but not assigned to any entity. Assign it or delete it.
compliance_messages.automation_no_description: Automation '{alias}' has no description. Add a 'description:' field to document its purpose.
compliance_messages.automation_no_unique_id: Automation '{alias}' has no 'id:' field. Without a unique id, this automation cannot be edited in the HA UI.
compliance_messages.script_no_description: Script '{alias}' has no description. Add a 'description:' field to document what it does.
compliance_messages.entity_no_area: Entity '{name}' ({entity_id}) has no area assigned. Assign it via Settings → Devices.
compliance_messages.entities_no_area_bulk: {count} physical entities have no area assigned. Assign areas via Settings → Devices & Services → Devices.

diag_prompts.step1_yaml: 1. Explain the issue in simple terms — why this is a bad practice and what could break
diag_prompts.step2_yaml: 2. Show me the exact YAML block causing the problem (📄 Current YAML:) and the proposed fix (✅ Proposed fixed YAML:)
diag_prompts.step3: 3. Present these options and wait for my answer before doing anything:
diag_prompts.step1_action: 1. Explain the issue in simple terms — why this is problematic and what could go wrong
diag_prompts.step2_action: 2. Describe precisely what you intend to do: {proposed}
diag_prompts.menu_title: What would you like to do?
diag_prompts.choice_backup_apply: 📋  Make a backup first, then apply
diag_prompts.choice_backup_proceed: 📋  Make a backup first, then proceed

problems.no_alias: it has no alias — its display name in the UI is the raw entity_id, unreadable
problems.no_description: it has no description — hard to maintain long-term
problems.never_triggered: it has never been triggered — it seems unused or misconfigured
problems.duplicate: it appears to be a duplicate of another automation
problems.device_id: it uses a device_id instead of an entity_id in a trigger/action/condition
problems.device_platform: it uses a deprecated "device" platform instead of a native trigger/condition
problems.deprecated_service: it calls a deprecated HA service
problems.unknown_service: it calls a service that cannot be found in HA
problems.unknown_area: it references an area that does not exist in HA
problems.unknown_label: it references a label that does not exist in HA
problems.mode_motion_single: it handles motion/presence but uses mode:single — it will miss triggers
problems.template_simple: it uses a Jinja2 template where a native HA trigger or condition would suffice
problems.wait_template: it uses wait_template when wait_for_trigger would be more efficient
problems.excessive_delay: it contains an excessive delay that blocks the HA execution thread
problems.blueprint_candidate: it matches a repetitive pattern — good candidate for blueprinting
problems.blueprint_missing: it references a blueprint whose file no longer exists
problems.blueprint_inputs: the blueprint it uses has missing or empty inputs
problems.empty_script: the script is empty — it has no actions
problems.script_orphan: the script is not called by any automation or other script
problems.script_cycle: the script creates an infinite loop by calling itself directly or indirectly
problems.script_depth: the script has a call chain that is too deep — risk of stack overflow or timeout
problems.script_single_mode: the script is in "single" mode but may be called in parallel or loop
problems.empty_scene: the scene is empty — it has no entities defined
problems.scene_duplicate: the scene is a duplicate of another existing scene
problems.scene_unavailable: the scene references an entity that is currently unavailable
problems.scene_not_triggered: the scene is not triggered by any automation
problems.zombie_entity: the entity is a ghost/zombie entry in the HA registry — its source integration no longer exists
problems.disabled_referenced: the entity is disabled but still referenced in one or more automations
problems.broken_device: the entity points to a device that no longer exists in the registry
problems.helper_unused: the helper is not used by any automation or script
problems.helper_disabled_only: the helper is only used by disabled automations
problems.helper_no_name: the helper has no friendly name — its raw ID is shown in the UI
problems.input_number_range: the input_number helper has an invalid min/max range (min ≥ max or incoherent step)
problems.input_select_duplicate: the input_select helper has duplicate options in its list
problems.input_select_empty: the input_select helper contains an empty option ("") in its list
problems.input_text_pattern: the input_text helper has an invalid or malformed regex pattern
problems.timer_zero: the timer has a duration of 0 — it fires instantly and serves no purpose
problems.timer_orphaned: the timer is not controlled by any automation
problems.timer_never_started: the timer has never been started since its creation
problems.dashboard_missing: a dashboard references entity "{eid}" which no longer exists
problems.hardcoded_secret: a secret (password, token, API key) is written in plain text in the config
problems.missing_state_class: the sensor has no state_class defined — HA cannot generate long-term statistics
problems.template_no_metadata: the template sensor has no device_class or unit_of_measurement
problems.template_no_unavail: the template does not handle the case where the source entity is unavailable (None)
problems.template_now_trigger: the automation uses now() in a template without a time trigger — the value will never update
problems.template_cycle: the template sensor creates a circular dependency with another template
problems.group_empty: the group is empty or all its member entities are unavailable
problems.group_missing: the group references entities that no longer exist
problems.group_nested: the group is nested too deeply (> 2 levels) — may cause performance issues
problems.zone_no_entity: the zone has no tracking entity (person/device_tracker) assigned
problems.unknown_floor: the automation references a floor that does not exist in HA
problems.compliance_no_name: the entity has no friendly name — its raw entity_id is shown in the UI
problems.compliance_no_uid: the automation has no unique_id — it cannot be edited via the HA interface
problems.area_high: the area has a high complexity score — too many entities/automations/scripts concentrated
problems.area_merge: two very similar areas with few entities could be merged
problems.redundancy_overlap: this automation logically overlaps another — same triggers, similar actions
problems.redundancy_blueprint: ≥3 automations are structurally identical — candidates for blueprinting
problems.redundancy_native: this automation reimplements functionality already built into HA natively

hints.no_alias: Suggest a readable alias based on what the automation does
hints.no_description: Suggest a clear description of what the automation does
hints.device_id: Show the trigger/action/condition block with the device_id AND the corrected block with the entity_id. Explain why a device_id breaks when hardware is replaced.
hints.device_platform: Show the current "device" block AND the equivalent native block (state, numeric_state, etc.)
hints.deprecated_service: Identify the obsolete service in the YAML and propose the current replacement service
hints.unknown_service: Identify the missing service and propose either the correct name or an alternative service
hints.unknown_area: Show the missing area reference and suggest existing areas as alternatives
hints.unknown_label: Show the missing label reference and suggest existing labels or creating the missing label
hints.mode_motion_single: Show the current "mode: single" block and the corrected YAML with "mode: restart". Explain the difference between single/restart/queued for motion automations.
hints.template_simple: Show the current template and the equivalent native trigger/condition. Explain why natives are preferred (performance, readability, reliability).
hints.wait_template: Show the current wait_template block and the equivalent wait_for_trigger block. Explain the resource consumption difference.
hints.excessive_delay: Identify the problematic delay block and propose a time trigger or wait_for_trigger instead
hints.blueprint_candidate: Explain what a blueprint is and why it simplifies maintenance
hints.blueprint_inputs: Show the blueprint definition and the missing inputs in the automation
hints.script_cycle: Identify the cyclic call path and show how to break it in the YAML
hints.script_depth: Show the call chain and propose a refactored flattened version
hints.script_single_mode: Explain single/queued/restart modes for scripts and show the corrected YAML with the right mode
hints.scene_unavailable: Identify the unavailable entity and propose either removing or replacing it
hints.zombie_entity: Explain what the HA entity registry is and why these ghost entries accumulate
hints.helper_unused: Explain the risks of keeping unused helpers (confusion, memory overhead)
hints.helper_no_name: Suggest a readable name based on the type and probable use of the helper
hints.input_number_range: Show the current configuration (min/max/step) and the corrected version
hints.input_select_duplicate: Show the current list with duplicates identified and the deduplicated proposed list
hints.input_select_empty: Show the current list and the cleaned list without the empty option
hints.input_text_pattern: Show the current pattern, explain the error, and propose either fixing or removing it
hints.timer_zero: Suggest a sensible duration (e.g. 00:05:00) or recommend removing the timer if unused
hints.dashboard_missing: Identify the card referencing this entity and propose either fixing the entity_id or removing the card
hints.hardcoded_secret: Show the relevant line (mask the sensitive value with ***) and the corrected YAML using !secret. Explain how secrets.yaml works in HA.
hints.missing_state_class: Explain the possible values (measurement, total, total_increasing) and which is appropriate for this sensor. Show the YAML with and without state_class.
hints.template_no_metadata: Suggest appropriate device_class and unit_of_measurement values based on the sensor's data type
hints.template_no_unavail: Show the current template and the safe version with "is not none" or "| default(0, true)" check. Explain why an entity can be None.
hints.template_now_trigger: Show the problematic now() template and propose either a time_pattern trigger or replacing now() with an input_datetime helper
hints.template_cycle: Identify the dependency loop and propose how to break it (direct value, intermediate helper, etc.)
hints.group_missing: Show the current members with missing entities identified and the corrected version without broken references
hints.group_nested: Show the current hierarchy and propose a flattened version with direct groups
hints.unknown_floor: Identify the missing floor reference and suggest existing floors or creating the missing floor
hints.compliance_no_name: Suggest a readable name based on the entity_id and the entity's domain
hints.compliance_no_uid: Explain why unique_id is required for UI editing. Show the current YAML and propose a unique_id based on the automation name.
hints.area_high: Explain the composite complexity concept and how to reduce it by separating areas
hints.area_merge: Explain the benefits of simplifying area structure in HA
hints.redundancy_overlap: Identify the duplicate automation, show both YAMLs side by side and propose a merged version
hints.redundancy_native: Identify the corresponding native feature (adaptive_lighting, schedule, alert…) and show how to enable it instead

proposed.never_triggered: check traces with ha_get_automation_traces, then delete if confirmed unused
proposed.duplicate: read both automations, compare their logic, then propose deleting or merging
proposed.blueprint_candidate: create a reusable blueprint with ha_create_blueprint and migrate this automation as the first instance
proposed.blueprint_missing: either import the missing blueprint, correct the path, or reconvert to a classic automation
proposed.empty_script: delete the script if unused, or add actions
proposed.script_orphan: confirm non-use with ha_deep_search, then delete if confirmed
proposed.empty_scene: delete the scene or add entities to it
proposed.scene_duplicate: compare both scenes and delete one
proposed.scene_not_triggered: check if it's used elsewhere (dashboards, scripts) then delete if truly unused
proposed.zombie_entity: verify no automation references it, then delete the orphan entry
proposed.disabled_referenced: either re-enable the entity, or update the automations that reference it
proposed.broken_device: either delete the orphan entry, or re-associate the entity to the correct device
proposed.helper_unused: confirm non-use with ha_deep_search, then delete if confirmed
proposed.helper_disabled_only: decide if the helper is still needed, then delete or keep
proposed.timer_orphaned: verify it is not triggered manually, then delete if confirmed unused
proposed.timer_never_started: check if an automation should start it, then delete if unused
proposed.group_empty: either delete the group, or update its members with valid entities
proposed.zone_no_entity: list available person entities and assign one or more to this zone
proposed.area_high: analyze the zone composition and propose splitting into logical sub-zones
proposed.area_merge: analyze both zones and propose how to merge them cleanly
proposed.redundancy_blueprint: create a generic blueprint and migrate each automation as an instance
proposed.script_orphan_hint: Explain how to detect orphan scripts and the risks of keeping them
proposed.blueprint_candidate_hint: Explain what a HA blueprint is and the benefits of centralized maintenance

redundancy.blueprint_problem: this automation is part of a group of ≥3 structurally identical automations — candidate for blueprinting
redundancy.blueprint_step1: 1. Explain what a HA blueprint is and why centralizing these automations simplifies maintenance
redundancy.blueprint_step2: 2. Show me the current YAML of this automation (📄 Current YAML:) and the proposed generic blueprint (✅ Proposed blueprint:)
redundancy.blueprint_choice_proceed: ✅  OK, create the blueprint and migrate the automations
redundancy.blueprint_choice_backup: 📋  Make a backup first, then create the blueprint
redundancy.native_problem: this automation manually reimplements a feature already natively built into HA: "{detail}"
redundancy.native_step1: 1. Explain why using the native HA feature is better (performance, auto-updates, less maintenance)
redundancy.native_step2: 2. Show me the current automation YAML (📄 Current YAML:) and how to enable the corresponding native feature (✅ Proposed native config:)
redundancy.overlap_problem: automations "{alias_a}" ({eid_a}) and "{alias_b}" have an identical trigger: "{trigger}" — they overlap logically
redundancy.overlap_step1: 1. Explain why two automations with the same trigger can conflict and how to consolidate them
redundancy.overlap_step2: 2. Show me both YAMLs side by side (📄 Automation A: / 📄 Automation B:) and the proposed merged version (✅ Consolidated YAML:)
redundancy.overlap_choice_backup: 📋  Make a backup first, then merge

area.cross_area_problem: this automation touches multiple areas ({areas}) — hard to maintain and debug
area.cross_area_step1: 1. Explain why it's better to have one automation per area (clarity, maintainability, easier debugging)
area.cross_area_step2: 2. Show me the current YAML (📄 Current YAML:) and how to split it into separate per-area automations (✅ Proposed YAML:)
area.cross_area_choice_backup: 📋  Make a backup first, then split
area.merge_problem: area "{area}" contains {count} simple automations (avg score {score}) with similar triggers — could be merged
area.merge_read:
→ List automations in this area with: ha_get_entities(area="{area}")
  then read each with: haca_get_automation(entity_id)
area.merge_step1: 1. Explain the pros and cons of merging automations (readability vs complexity)
area.merge_step2: 2. Show me the current YAMLs and a merged version using choose/conditions (✅ Proposed merged YAML:)
area.merge_choice_backup: 📋  Make a backup first, then merge
area.split_problem: area "{area}" contains complex automations (avg score {score}) that mix too many responsibilities
area.split_read:
→ List complex automations with: ha_get_entities(area="{area}")
  then read each with: haca_get_automation(entity_id)
area.split_step1: 1. Explain the single-responsibility principle applied to HA automations (one automation = one behavior)
area.split_step2: 2. Show me the current YAML (📄 Current YAML:) and propose splitting into separate automations per feature (✅ Proposed YAML:)
area.split_choice_backup: 📋  Make a backup first, then split

analyzer.trigger_uses_device_id: Trigger {idx} uses device_id which breaks when device is re-added
analyzer.condition_uses_device_id: Condition {idx} uses device_id which breaks when device is re-added
analyzer.action_uses_device_id: Action {idx} uses device_id which breaks when device is re-added
analyzer.action_target_uses_device_id: Action {idx} uses device_id in target which breaks when device is re-added
analyzer.trigger_uses_device_platform: Trigger {idx} uses 'device' platform which is fragile
analyzer.condition_uses_device_platform: Condition {idx} uses 'device' condition type which is fragile
analyzer.template_simple_state: Condition {idx} uses template for simple state check
analyzer.template_numeric_comparison: Condition {idx} uses template for numeric comparison
analyzer.template_time_check: Condition {idx} uses template for time check
analyzer.wait_template_state_check: Action {idx} uses wait_template for state check
analyzer.motion_single_mode: Motion automation uses 'single' mode with delays/waits
analyzer.never_triggered: Automation has never been triggered since HA restart
analyzer.script_no_sequence: Script '{alias}' has no sequence of actions
analyzer.add_description: Add a description to document automation purpose
analyzer.add_alias: Add an alias to make automation identifiable
analyzer.use_entity_id_instead_device: Use entity_id instead of device_id for reliability
analyzer.use_state_platform: Use state/numeric_state platform with entity_id instead
analyzer.use_state_condition: Use native 'state' condition instead of template
analyzer.use_numeric_state_condition: Use 'numeric_state' condition for validation at load time
analyzer.use_entity_id_in_target: Use entity_id in target instead of device_id
analyzer.use_wait_for_trigger: Use wait_for_trigger with state trigger (event-driven, not polling)
analyzer.use_restart_mode: Use 'restart' mode - re-triggers must reset the timer
analyzer.verify_triggers: Verify triggers are configured correctly or automation is needed
analyzer.specify_zone_entity: Specify which entity to track in the zone
analyzer.entity_referenced_not_exist: Referenced by {count} automation(s) but doesn't exist
analyzer.orphaned_registry_entry: Orphaned registry entry: integration '{platform}' is missing or disabled
analyzer.remove_orphaned: Remove this orphaned entity from your registry
analyzer.use_entity_id_or_update: Use entity_id or update to a valid device
analyzer.entity_may_be_broken: Entity may be broken or no longer in use
analyzer.triggered_recently_loop: Triggered very recently ({delta}s ago), possible loop
analyzer.complex_automation: Automation has {count} actions, which is complex
analyzer.parallel_mode_resources: Parallel mode with max={max_runs} can consume resources
analyzer.triggers_modifies_same: Automation triggers on and modifies the same entities: {entities}
analyzer.missing_state_class: Power/Energy sensor missing state_class, impacting long-term stats
analyzer.expensive_template_no_domain: Template uses 'states | selectattr' without domain filter — re-evaluates on every state change
analyzer.add_domain_filter: Add a domain filter (e.g. 'states.sensor | selectattr(...)') to limit evaluation scope
analyzer.expensive_template_states_all: Template lists all entity states — extremely expensive, re-evaluates on every state change
analyzer.filter_by_domain: Filter states by domain (e.g. 'states.light | list') to reduce template evaluation cost
analyzer.hardcoded_secret: Potential hardcoded secret or API key detected
analyzer.sensitive_data_exposure: Action {idx}: Potential exposure of sensitive data in notification
analyzer.use_secret_yaml: Use !secret in secrets.yaml instead of hardcoding sensitive data
analyzer.avoid_secrets_notifications: Avoid sending secrets or passwords in notification messages
analyzer.duplicate_automation: Automation has {count} duplicate(s) with identical triggers and actions
analyzer.excessive_delay: Action {idx} has an excessive delay of {minutes} minutes (>30 min)
analyzer.use_input_number_delay: Consider using an input_number helper to make the delay configurable
analyzer.blueprint_missing_path: Blueprint reference is missing a path
analyzer.specify_blueprint_path: Specify the blueprint path in use_blueprint.path
analyzer.blueprint_file_not_found: Blueprint file not found on disk: blueprints/automation/{path}
analyzer.reinstall_blueprint: Reinstall or restore the blueprint file at blueprints/automation/{path}
analyzer.configure_blueprint_inputs: Configure the required blueprint inputs in use_blueprint.input
analyzer.blueprint_empty_input: Blueprint input '{key}' is empty or null
analyzer.set_blueprint_input: Set a value for blueprint input '{key}'
analyzer.unused_input_boolean: Input boolean '{entity_id}' is not referenced in any automation or script
analyzer.remove_unused_helper: Consider removing this unused helper to clean up your configuration
analyzer.unknown_service: Action {idx} calls service "{service}" which is not registered in Home Assistant
analyzer.use_existing_service: Verify that the integration providing "{service}" is installed and loaded. Check for typos.
analyzer.unknown_area_reference: Action {idx} targets area_id "{area_id}" which no longer exists in your Areas registry
analyzer.check_area_name: Go to Settings → Areas & Zones to check if "{area_id}" was renamed or deleted, then update this automation.
analyzer.unknown_floor_reference: Action {idx} targets floor_id "{floor_id}" which no longer exists in your Floors registry
analyzer.check_floor_name: Go to Settings → Areas & Zones → Floors to check if "{floor_id}" was renamed or deleted.
analyzer.unknown_label_reference: Action {idx} targets label_id "{label_id}" which no longer exists in your Labels registry
analyzer.check_label_name: Go to Settings → Labels to check if "{label_id}" was renamed or deleted.
analyzer.ghost_automation: Ghost automation: active but not triggered in {days} days
analyzer.ghost_unavailable_triggers: Ghost automation: trigger entities are unavailable ({entities})
analyzer.fix_unavailable_triggers: Check or replace the unavailable trigger entities — likely root cause
analyzer.verify_triggers_restrictive: Check if triggers are too restrictive, or if the automation is still needed
analyzer.probable_duplicate_automation: Probable duplicate at {pct}% similarity with "{other}" (Jaccard of triggers/actions)
analyzer.review_probable_duplicate: Compare with "{other}" and merge or remove the duplicate

| Date | Score |
|------|-------|
| {ref_date} | **{ref_score}%** |
| Today | **{cur_score}%** |

Open the **H.A.C.A panel → History** tab to identify the new issues and restore your configuration quality.

---
*H.A.C.A — Home Assistant Config Auditor*

analyzer.script_single_mode_loop: Script '{alias}' has mode:single but is called by rapid-trigger automation '{automation}'

analyzer.script_blueprint_candidate: Script '{alias}' is called by {count} automations — a blueprint would improve reusability
analyzer.script_create_blueprint: Convert to a blueprint: it can be reused with different parameters

analyzer.group_all_unavailable: All members of group '{entity_id}' are unavailable

conversation.fallback_intro: Could not reach an advanced AI assistant (OpenAI/Gemini), here is a local analysis:

impacts.device_id: Using device_id instead of entities weakens your automation — replacing hardware will break the link.
impacts.unavailable: This entity no longer exists or is offline, which can block your automations or dashboards.
impacts.zombie: This entity no longer exists or is offline, which can block your automations or dashboards.
impacts.mode: The single execution mode risks dropping triggers if the automation is already running.
impacts.secret: Sensitive data is exposed in plaintext — this is a security risk.
impacts.security: Sensitive data is exposed in plaintext — this is a security risk.
impacts.default: This issue may affect the stability or performance of your system.

{context}

ai_prompts.fallback_impact_unavailable: This entity no longer exists or is offline, which can block your automations.

ai_prompts.complexity_prompt:
You are a Home Assistant expert. Analyse the complexity of this {kind}.

** {kind_upper} :** {alias}
**Entity ID :** {entity_id}
**HACA Score :** {score}
**Score detail :** {score_line}

{yaml_section}

MISSION IN 2 PARTS:
PART 1 - EXPLANATION (3-5 sentences):
Explain why this {kind} is complex, which parts contribute most to the score, and the risks (maintenance, debugging).

PART 2 - YAML PROPOSAL:
Propose a concrete split: extract repetitive action blocks into separate scripts, simplify the main file. Provide the full YAML.

RESPOND STRICTLY WITH THIS FORMAT:
```explanation
[your explanation here]
```

```yaml_proposal
[proposed yaml here]
```
ai_prompts.complexity_fallback: Complexity score: {score}. This {kind} contains {triggers} trigger(s), {conditions} condition(s), {actions} recursive action(s) and {templates} template(s). Consider extracting repetitive action blocks into reusable scripts.

compliance_messages.helper_no_area: Helper '{name}' has no area. Assign it in Settings → Helpers.

llm_prompt.system_role: You are HACA, an expert Home Assistant configuration assistant.
llm_prompt.rule_backup: Before any modification (create, edit, delete), call ha_backup_create.
llm_prompt.rule_explain: Explain what you will do BEFORE doing it.
llm_prompt.rule_confirm: For destructive operations, ask user confirmation.
llm_prompt.rule_use_tools: Use available tools to read, analyze, and modify the HA configuration.
llm_prompt.rule_proactive: Be PROACTIVE: if you need information you can get via a tool, call the tool instead of asking the user. For example, to know dashboard views, call ha_get_lovelace.
llm_prompt.workflow_lovelace:
1. ha_list_dashboards() to list dashboards
2. ha_get_lovelace(dashboard_id) to see views and cards
3. ha_add_lovelace_card(card, dashboard_id) to add a card
   - If only 1 view, use view_index=0 without asking.
   - Card types are ALWAYS in English: weather-forecast, entities, button, light, thermostat, glance, gauge, tile, area, sensor, etc.
   - If user says 'weather'/'météo'/'Wetter'/etc., use type='weather-forecast'.
4. ha_update_lovelace_card() / ha_remove_lovelace_card() to modify/delete
llm_prompt.workflow_auto:
1. ha_get_entities(domain='automation') to list
2. haca_get_automation(entity_id) to read full YAML
3. ha_create_automation() / ha_update_automation() to create/modify
llm_prompt.workflow_scripts:
1. ha_get_entities(domain='script') to list
2. ha_get_script(entity_id) to read
3. ha_create_script() / ha_update_script() to create/modify
